title: Hot Water Beach Review
meta_desc: Discover the unique Hot Water Beach in Coromandel, NZ. Free admission, suitable for all ages, and a one-of-a-kind experience. Read our honest family review.
intro: Hot Water Beach is a must-visit destination for families in New Zealand. This unique beach offers a free and fun experience for all ages. With geothermal water seeping through the sand, you can dig your own hot pool and enjoy the warmth.

sections.heading: What to Expect
sections.body:
Hot Water Beach is a truly unique experience, especially for kids. The geothermal water seeping through the sand is warm and inviting, and digging your own hot pool is a fun activity for the whole family. However, be careful as the water can reach 64°C in some areas, so make sure to mix in cold seawater to avoid burns.

The best time to visit is within 2 hours either side of low tide, so be sure to check the tide times before heading out. The beach can get crowded during peak season, but the experience is still enjoyable.
list: Hire a spade for $5 to dig your own hot pool
Be careful of hot water and mix in cold seawater

sections.heading: Age Suitability and Value for Money
sections.body:
Hot Water Beach is suitable for all ages, from 0+. The experience is free, although you'll need to hire a spade for $5. Considering the unique experience and the fact that it's free, Hot Water Beach offers excellent value for money.

Families with young children should be aware that the hot water can be a hazard, so close supervision is necessary. However, with proper care, Hot Water Beach is an excellent destination for families with kids of all ages.

sections.heading: Insider Tips and Best Time to Visit
sections.body:
To make the most of your visit, arrive early to secure a good spot on the beach. Bring a picnic lunch and enjoy it on the beach while you soak in your hot pool. Don't forget to bring sunscreen, towels, and a change of clothes.

The best time to visit Hot Water Beach is during the summer months when the weather is warm and sunny. However, be prepared for crowds during peak season. If you prefer a more relaxed experience, consider visiting during the shoulder season.

sections.heading: Booking and Saving Money
sections.body: There's no need to book in advance, as admission to Hot Water Beach is free. Simply show up at the beach within 2 hours either side of low tide and hire a spade for $5. You can save money by bringing your own picnic lunch and avoiding the nearby cafes and restaurants.
